stdout:
I’ve got an initial review signal: there’s at least one likely blocking `--json` contract bug. I’m pulling the exact diff and surrounding code now so I can verify it precisely and cite the right paths/lines.

I found the main serve implementation files. Next I’m reading the command wiring, server daemon logic, and tests together so I can verify both the feature and the one suspected contract violation.

I’ve confirmed the serve branch itself. I’m checking the remaining acceptance-criteria surfaces now: hidden schema exposure, `--web-dir` server support, and whether the new public API docs meet the repo’s doc standard.

## Acceptance criteria

`jit serve` is mostly implemented correctly: detached start/stop/status live in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:226-361`, `--web-dir` is wired in `crates/server/src/main.rs:37-42,92-95`, and MCP hiding is present in `crates/jit/src/schema.rs:147-190` (`"serve"` at line 187).

Blocking: `--json` is not fully implemented. In `crates/jit/src/main.rs:3564-3566`, `3602-3604`, and `3658-3660`, serve errors always go to plain `stderr`, so `jit serve --json` does not produce machine-readable output on failure.

## Architecture and separation of concerns

No blocking boundary violation observed. Lifecycle/process logic stays in `commands/serve.rs`, CLI wiring/output stays in `main.rs`, and static-file serving stays in `crates/server/src/main.rs`.

## Functional paradigm

No blocking issue observed. The new port-selection logic in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:137-145` uses iterators appropriately.

## Correctness and invariants

Blocking: PID safety is incomplete in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:334-337`. `stop_server` converts `u32` to `i32` but does not reject `0`, so a corrupted/manual PID file with `pid: 0` would call `kill(0, SIGTERM)`, signaling the whole process group. `is_process_alive` correctly guards `<= 0` at `107-121`; `stop_server` should apply the same protection.

Atomic PID-file writes are correctly implemented in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:73-80`.

## Safety

No `unsafe` usage observed. The PID handling bug above is still a blocking safety issue.

## Testing

New unit coverage exists in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:375-563`, including stale PID cleanup and `u32::MAX` handling. But there is no test covering the broken `--json` error paths in `main.rs`, and no test asserting `stop_server` rejects PID `0`.

## Error handling

`commands/serve.rs` generally returns contextual `Result`s, but the CLI layer regresses structured error handling for `serve` by bypassing `JsonError`/`handle_json_error!` in the three error branches noted above.

## CLI contract

Blocking failure: `--json` success paths are present, but failure paths are not JSON. That violates the repo-wide CLI contract and the issue acceptance criteria.

## Documentation

Blocking technical debt: the new public API surface in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:20-352` has doc comments, but the public items do not include the required `# Examples` sections (`ServerPidFile`, `ServeOutcome`, `StopOutcome`, and the exported helper functions).

## Prior review feedback

`run_history` is empty, so there is no prior review feedback to verify.

## Jit issue management

`issue.dependencies` is empty, so there are no prerequisites to check. I could not independently run `jit validate` in this environment because the tool invocation was denied, but the blocking issues above already require the review to fail.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
